ending: The mayor's attempt to kill Grandmother Lu and her household ends Sen's willingness to leave the Orchard's Reach feud unresolved. With Falling Leaf, he infiltrates the mayor's estate, incapacitates its guards and workers, and kills the mayor and Jiang Hao. Falling Leaf kills the mayor's wife after judging her denial of involvement false. Grandmother Lu survives with her company, staff, fighting fans, possible advancement medicine, and the gold Sen gives her. She later separates from Sen's eastbound caravan to travel south. Falling Leaf returns to the mountain because human society remains dangerous for her, though she intends to watch over Grandmother Lu at times and may reunite with Sen. Feng and Kho remain behind to investigate the mountain's abnormal spirit-beast violence, while Caihong expects Sen to write. Sen defeats a powerful metal-attuned spirit beast while guarding the caravan, treats his own wounds, and reaches the eastern city connected to the call he has sensed. Its purpose is still unknown. He also continues to owe Laughing River a favor, may face retaliation from a slain cultivator's clan, and expects the freed indebted fighter to meet him on the road.
in_short: Sen begins as a homeless boy in Orchard's Reach, protected only by Grandmother Lu. The cultivator Feng Ming chooses him as a disciple and takes him to Kho Jaw-Long's mountain home, where Feng, Kho, Ma Caihong, and the spirit beast Falling Leaf become his adopted family and teachers. Years of martial, medical, and cultivation training carry Sen into middle Foundation Formation, but also expose him to the violence of the Jianghu. He returns home before following an unexplained pull toward the east and finds Grandmother Lu restored to health and prosperous. The mayor's family escalates an old grievance into repeated murder attempts. Sen and Falling Leaf kill the mayor, his wife, and Jiang Hao while sparing uninvolved workers. Falling Leaf returns to the mountain, Grandmother Lu turns south with her caravan, and Sen travels east as a guard. After surviving one final spirit-beast attack, he reaches the city that has been calling him.

recaps.text: Sen survives on the streets of Orchard's Reach by stealing and scavenging, sharing what he can with Grandmother Lu, the only person he considers family. When the visiting cultivator Feng announces that he will choose a local disciple, the mayor excludes poor children and presents favored youths instead. Feng discovers Sen in hiding, selects him, and punishes the mayor. Because the rejected nobles may kill Sen after such a humiliation, Feng insists that the boy cannot remain in town. Sen agrees to leave only after Feng gives Grandmother Lu a secure home, money, and medicine. She formally claims Sen as family and gives him the name Lu Sen. On the mountain journey, Feng learns that his new disciple needs ordinary meals and adjusts his pace and route. Sen also befriends a camouflaging Ghost Panther by sharing food with her. Feng promises that Sen will not be punished for voicing necessary concerns. He then gives Sen a pill that begins an agonizing purification of his body.

The purification leaves Sen leaner, stronger, and able to sense energy-rich food. He keeps pace more easily and glimpses the wider world from the mountainside before reaching a secluded manor. Its airborne defender proves to be Feng's old friend Kho Jaw-Long, who welcomes Sen as a nephew. Kho identifies the boy's earlier cleansing as an earthly transformation and confirms that Sen possesses an unusual ability to hide his presence. Once Sen admits that he cannot read, Kho undertakes his education and also teaches him basic cooking. Feng handles self-defense and physical conditioning. Across the seasons, Sen develops literacy, endurance, strikes, blocks, and controlled martial forms, while the Ghost Panther runs beside him and becomes a steady companion. By the next New Year, Sen can read independently, prepare meals, and perform Feng's foundational form with enough precision to stop a blow just short of his master. Feng releases him from close supervision of the basics. Kho gives him a spear, Feng gives him a straight sword, and Sen accepts a second cleansing pill as his training moves into more dangerous territory.

Sen uses the second cleansing to open more internal channels, leaving his energy reserve depleted. While he recovers, Kho's history lessons show him that neither ordinary society nor cultivation offers complete safety. The Ghost Panther guides him toward the power gathered near his navel. Sen secretly experiments with it, learning to circulate it but not how to replenish it. During a severe training drill, Feng exhausts that reserve and finally recognizes the misunderstanding. He identifies it as Sen's lower dantian, names its energy qi, and teaches him to draw power from the world before lasting damage occurs. Feng accepts responsibility for withholding foundational knowledge, while Sen admits that embarrassment led him to conceal his experiments. Two more years pass. Sen cultivates while moving, learns that qi has varied attributes, and develops access to an exceptionally broad range, with fire and shadow his strongest affinities. Feng requires him to train the weaker ones as well. The Ghost Panther reveals her name as Falling Leaf. With Sen's basic cultivation established, Feng announces that the next task is developing killing intent.

Feng demonstrates killing intent by overwhelming Sen without touching him, then proposes sending him across the mountain to acquire it through real danger. Kho strongly objects, and his wife Ma Caihong arrives to demand an independent assessment. Her argument with Feng exposes an old grievance but not its cause. Caihong tests Sen's sword work, patience, qi techniques, and concealment, then helps equip him with medicine and a storage ring. Feng kills many of the mountain's strongest spirit beasts before the trial, an act intended to improve Sen's odds. Sen sets out for a cave on the far side, and Falling Leaf follows. He kills attacking spirit beasts, discovers that Caihong's treatment opened previously blocked channels, and begins combining qi techniques despite the strain. Falling Leaf saves him from a falling hawk and guards him while he heals. Over several days their combat routine sharpens his weaker affinities and fills his ring with beast cores. When a white ape strikes Falling Leaf down, Sen believes she has been killed. His desire to avenge her finally forms killing intent, which he combines with fire qi to kill the ape.

Falling Leaf survives the ape's blow. Sen tends her with Caihong's emergency medicine, learns to direct his new killing intent, and completes the mountain crossing. Feng is troubled that normally cautious spirit beasts attacked so aggressively and accepts responsibility for investigating. Back at the manor, Sen reconciles with Caihong and accepts her lessons in medicine, field remedies, sword misdirection, and control of killing intent. He begins calling her Auntie Caihong, and that recognition of family triggers a major breakthrough. Sen condenses his first liquid qi and simultaneously completes qi condensation and earthly transformation, entering Foundation Formation in both spirit and body cultivation. His teachers finally explain the formal stages and their own specialties: Feng centers on combat, Caihong on healing and alchemy, and Kho on formations. During further field study, Sen meets the red spirit fox Laughing River. She trades a rare blossom for his promise of a future favor and sends greetings to Caihong. Falling Leaf also reveals that she can speak through qi. At New Year, Sen delivers the flower and message, while his teachers give him a preservation ring, a liquid-qi manual, and durable writing tools for his eventual travels.

Caihong judges Sen capable of emergency stabilization but not a spirit doctor. Kho begins teaching him the spear and formations, emphasizing balance and changing conditions. Sen adopts a foundation method that condenses all the qi attributes he can use rather than forcing him into one specialty. His body and qi reserves strengthen, and he considers several possible paths without choosing one. The mountain's abnormal spirit-beast behavior remains under investigation. When three core formation cultivators pursue Feng home and threaten Sen, Kho kills two and sends the survivor back to the Diving Falcon Sect with a warning. The incident forces Sen's teachers to explain the Jianghu, the informal world of cultivator reputation, challenges, controlled violence, and feuds. Feng's reputation attracts conflict, while guest obligations required Kho to answer a threat against Sen. Each elder explains why cultivation once appealed to them, but none can promise that violence is avoidable. Falling Leaf tells Sen that he will fight when forced, just as an animal defends itself. Sen decides not to abandon cultivation. Instead, he asks for realistic preparation against wounds, ambushes, groups, and situations where escape matters more than victory.

Months of harsh group combat teach Sen to function through pain, exhaustion, distraction, and attacks from several directions. He improves his spiritual awareness, accepts healing pills when necessary, and learns to maintain several qi patterns at once. During this period he senses an unexplained call from the east that seems connected to the ocean. He decides to leave in spring, first visiting Grandmother Lu and then seeking a practical route east. Feng, Kho, and Caihong affirm that the manor remains his home while preparing him for independence with contacts, travel warnings, lightning qi, spear refinement, and qinggong. Falling Leaf refuses his invitation to accompany him because humans would hunt her, but leaves open the possibility of meeting again. Another breakthrough carries Sen to middle Foundation Formation. His teachers give him travel robes, a map, a spirit-level sword, money, medicine, and other supplies. Caihong includes a sealed preparation for a future attempt at core formation, and Kho sends a package to Grandmother Lu. Sen finally departs the mountain for Orchard's Reach. Feng and Kho remain behind to investigate the spirit-beast disturbance, while Feng quietly arranges for others to watch over his disciple from a distance.

Sen and Falling Leaf part at the forest edge, and he enters Orchard's Reach while concealing his strength. Grandmother Lu now has servants and a large, successful trading company. She appears decades younger because Feng's medicine repaired meridians damaged by her cultivator family in childhood and restored her ability to cycle qi. After being discarded by that family, she lived as a mortal and later fell into poverty. She built the new business with Feng's money and her restored health so Sen would have support across the region. Jiang Hao soon confronts Sen with six qi-condensing associates. Sen defeats the attackers without killing them. A large unarmed cultivator refuses to join the assault because he serves only under a family debt. Sen promises to arrange the payment through Grandmother Lu so the man can become a wanderer. Reflecting on the public fear caused by his strength, Sen resolves to use power for protection rather than indulgence. The insight triggers a breakthrough. He reaches Grandmother Lu's home and takes the dangerous opportunity to stretch his dantian and channels, using a self-made elixir to reinforce them. The alteration greatly expands his possible qi flow, though its cost is not yet clear.

Sen wakes with wider channels and a larger dantian, but his existing qi now fills only part of the expanded space. He uses a formation and spirit-beast cores to accelerate recovery, drawing on lessons from Kho and Caihong. Grandmother Lu helps sell ordinary cores discreetly and completes the payment that frees the indebted fighter. A Foundation Formation blade cultivator, Jiang Hao's teacher, then demands a death duel in the market. Sen fights cautiously until the man threatens Grandmother Lu's employees. Sen answers with killing intent and kills him, then takes his weapons, money, seal, and concealed storage ring as spoils. He publicly directs suspicion toward the mayor, hoping to weaken the family without an immediate assault. The captured ring contains valuable equipment and a large quantity of gold that Grandmother Lu believes belongs to the mayor. She expects its loss to provoke violence that very night. Sen gives her pills from Feng and Caihong that may permit a breakthrough to Foundation Formation, as well as Kho's gift of sharpened fighting fans suited to her old training. Returning the gold will not ensure safety, so they prepare for an attack.

Sen waits at Grandmother Lu's shop behind an alarm formation and detects five intruders. Using concealing shadows, he kills most of the attackers and captures a surviving Foundation Formation cultivator, who identifies the mayor as their employer. Sen pays her to leave town and warns her never to return or threaten Grandmother Lu. He then discovers that a second group has attacked the house. Falling Leaf has returned and protects the injured senior servant, while Grandmother Lu defends the maid. Sen kills three assailants and Grandmother Lu defeats the last. After stabilizing the servant with an elixir, Sen decides the mayor must die. Grandmother Lu agrees but insists that guards, servants, and other uninvolved people must be spared. Sen and Falling Leaf enter the mayor's estate under cover of shadow, disabling guards and sedating workers rather than treating everyone present as an enemy. Sen confronts and kills the mayor. Falling Leaf kills the mayor's wife after judging her claim of ignorance false, and Sen kills Jiang Hao when he promises lifelong revenge. The mayor's immediate family is dead, while most estate workers remain alive.

With the feud ended, Sen accepts that Orchard's Reach no longer holds him. Falling Leaf returns to the mountain because remaining among humans would expose her to hunters, though she agrees to check on Grandmother Lu from time to time. Sen gives Grandmother Lu enough gold for lasting security and joins her eastbound caravan as a guard. The mayor's death has destabilized local government, but the guards do not attempt to arrest a wandering cultivator they cannot practically control. Before departure, Sen proves his value to the caravan's body-cultivator captain and obtains a favored bun recipe from a local family. Grandmother Lu plans to turn south with the caravan, while Sen continues east toward the unexplained pull he has sensed for months. On the road, a powerful metal-attuned spirit beast attacks. Sen kills it with an improvised lightning-infused spear strike, sustaining multiple wounds in the explosion. His medical training lets him treat himself and recover during the remainder of the journey. He finally arrives at the eastern city that has been calling him, independent and well supplied, with the purpose of that summons still unknown.
